question_title: Which Bloc will form the 2026 Danish Government?
summary: The 2026 Danish election produced a highly fragmented parliament with neither the red bloc (84 seats) nor blue bloc (77 seats) reaching the 90-seat majority threshold. The Moderates (14 seats) are the kingmaker. Frederiksen is the royal investigator exploring an S+SF+Radikale+Moderates government (Red Bloc). As of April 2-3, blue parties have been excluded from talks, but major obstacles remain: Moderates declare wealth tax "100% dead" and oppose Enhedslisten dependence. Løkke is publicly pushing a cross-bloc center alternative (S+V+K+M). The timeline is tight - 37 days from election to May deadline, compared to the 42-day record in 2022, with this parliament being far more fragmented. Red Bloc is the most likely single outcome given it's the active negotiation track, but No Formation before May has substantial probability given the difficulty and timeline. Cross Bloc is a meaningful fallback scenario being prepared in the background. Blue Bloc is near-impossible due to internal divisions and seat math.

factors.description: Frederiksen is actively exploring S+SF+R+M (Red Bloc) government - this is the primary track, blue parties excluded from talks as of April 1
DR analyst Rikke Gjøl Mansø identifies S+SF+R+M as most realistic coalition option
Under negative parliamentarism, even S+SF+R without Moderates (68 seats) could survive if Moderates abstain - only 77 blue votes against < 90 threshold
Moderates demand wealth tax dropped (Løkke: '100% dead') and oppose Enhedslisten dependence - major obstacles to S+SF+R+M deal
Løkke (Moderates) publicly pushing for cross-bloc center government (S+V+K+M) on April 2 Facebook post; Berlingske discusses V+K conditions for joining S
Venstre ruled out governing with Frederiksen ('Either centre-right or opposition'); Konservative ruled out government with SF/Radikale
Cross-bloc is the natural fallback if red bloc talks fail - Berlingske and Løkke already preparing ground for this scenario
Blue bloc only 77 seats, internal divisions (DF and Danmarksdemokraterne refuse to work with Løkke) make blue-only government nearly impossible
Most fragmented parliament in modern Danish history with 12 parties, deep policy divisions, Easter break delays talks
2022 government took record 42 days to form (would be May 5 from this election) - current situation is arguably harder with more parties and deeper divisions
Geopolitical pressure (Trump/Greenland crisis, economic uncertainty) creates incentive to resolve government formation faster
10 days into talks with no breakthrough but 27 days remain - tight but achievable timeline for Danish coalition formation

reasoning: The reviewer flagged a minor embellishment in one factor (Monocle citation) but confirmed the overall analysis is sound. Let me finalize my forecast.

resolution_criteria:
This question will resolve as the Bloc makeup of the Danish Government following the 2026 election based on the following criteria:

* Red Bloc, if all government parties other than the Moderates belong to the red bloc.
* Blue Bloc, if all government parties other than the Moderates belong to the blue bloc.
* Cross Bloc, if government parties belong to both blocs.

If no government is formed before May 2026, this question will resolve as No Formation before May.
fine_print:
Transitional/caretaker governments will not qualify for this question.

If a new election is called before a government is formed, this question will immediately resolve as No Formation before May.

This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print of the linked original question. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
